seasonArc: Jhonatan Narváez's 2026 became the season he turned into UAE Team Emirates-XRG's irreplaceable stage-hunter, and the Giro d'Italia was the stage on which he proved it. After the team's Bulgarian crash disaster gutted its roster — Vine, Soler, Adam Yates and Baroncini all out — Narváez carried the campaign almost single-handedly, sprinting from a reduced group to win Stage 4 in Cosenza, then doubling up on Stage 8 in Fermo and Stage 11 in Chiavari for a remarkable three stage wins by the race's final week. The Ecuadorian's versatility was the through-line: he won from breakaways and on punchy uphill finishes alike, the kind of all-terrain opportunism that also kept him in the fight for the maglia ciclamino against pure sprinter Paul Magnier. The trio of victories pushed his Giro career tally to five individual stages across 2020, 2024 and 2026, cementing him as one of the most prolific stage-hunters in the modern peloton. With the Giro running to its Rome finish on 31 May, Narváez had already authored UAE's defining storyline of the race and set himself up as a marked man for the breakaways and one-day classics to come.
